message:
connectors + google-discovery: tighten SSRF + credential-shape validation

Hardens two independent config-parsing surfaces against the same
class of issue: attacker-controlled URL strings in configure payloads
being allowed through to the HTTP client. Defense-in-depth before the
actual request is issued.

notion/connector.rs:
- Introduce optional_nonempty_string() to reject token/credential_id
  values that are present-but-empty-string (previously silently
  accepted, leading to downstream auth-header "Bearer " requests).
- Introduce validate_api_url_for_auth(): when running in direct-token
  mode (NotionAuth::Token), api_url must target https://api.notion.com
  or a localhost dev endpoint - preventing a malicious configure
  payload from redirecting token-bearing requests to an attacker host.
  In credential-id mode (vault-bound), any https origin is allowed
  since the vault proxy itself enforces the destination.
- Introduce optional_safe_cursor() with MAX_NOTION_CURSOR_BYTES=512
  cap and control-character rejection, applied to start_cursor so
  pagination opaque cursors cannot be used to smuggle log-injection
  bytes or trigger unbounded allocation.
- Corresponding test coverage: reject empty tokens, reject custom
  api_url under direct-token mode with an api.notion.com assertion
  in the error message, allow custom api_url under credential_id mode.

fcp-google-discovery/src/auth.rs:
- validate_token_url() tightened from "https OR localhost" to a
  closed allowlist: must be https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token
  with no query, no fragment, no userinfo. Localhost test hosts
  are still permitted but also lose the query/fragment escape.
- Rejects subtle bypasses like https://oauth2.googleapis.com.attacker.example/token,
  https://user:pass@oauth2.googleapis.com/token, and
  https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token?state=evil.

Changes authored by other concurrent agents in the working tree;
captured here in a single commit rather than stashed or reverted,
per project convention on multi-agent workspaces.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
